calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Continued To Apr-04-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant General Refractories Company Llc
Off Calendar Nov-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default judgment prove-up hearing as to Defendant General Refactories Company Inc.

Dept. 505: Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:06:05 a.m. and concluding at 10:20:00 a.m. (D505)

Jason Rose, Esq. appearing for and on behalf of Plaintiff Donald Teeter.
Defendant General Refactories Company Inc. not appearing.

Plaintiff's complaint for personal injury filed January 8, 2021.
Proof of Personal service as to Defendant General Refactories Company LLC Filed August 11, 2022.
Default entered as to Defendant General Refactories Company LLC on October 12, 2022.
Application for entry of default judgment against Defendant General Refactories Company LLC filed on August 24, 2023.
Proof of service of the notice of default hearing filed September 11, 2023.
Order waiving personal appearance filed October 24, 2023.

Plaintiff's counsel to summarize facts of the case and provides offer of proof.

Plaintiff's counsel moves the following exhibits into the evidence
A. Declaration of James P. Nevin
B. Medical Report Medical Report of Richard A. Levy, M.D.
C. Proof of Default, Service of Summons, and Damages Ceiling Request for Default Judgment, Proof of Service and Statement of Damages as to GENERAL REFRACTORIES COMPANY LLC
D. Operative Complaint upon which the Defendant was Defaulted Complaint, filed January 8, 2021; Summons
E. Non-Economic Damages (Personal Injury) Declaration of Plaintiff
F. Non-Economic Damages Declaration of James P. Nevin
G. Work history Worksite Product identification and summary of workplace exposure
H. Future Medical Damages Declaration of Frank Ganzhorn, M.D.
I. DOE Amendment Amendment to Complaint to identify DOE 3 as Defendant GENERAL REFRACTORIES COMPANY LLC

The Court accepts the exhibits as evidence.
The Court examines Plaintiff's counsel.
The matter is submitted.

After reviewing all the evidence, hearing the statements and testimony, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff Donald Teeter and against Defendant GENERAL REFRACTORIES COMPANY LLC as follows:

The Plaintiff shall recover from the Defendant the following:

Damages $826,750.00
Interest $0
Costs $0
Attorney's Fees $0

Judgment signed and filed this day.

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2023 Continued To Apr-04-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Advanced From Sep-12-22 To Jun-13-22 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Oct-04-2022 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-06-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Apr-25-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Saleem Belbahri of POND NORTH LLP (213-617-6169), appearing for defendant, Amcord, Inc.
Michelle Golden of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant, Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor Inc.
Paul S. Atigapramoj of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300 x.320), appearing for defendant, Foster Wheeler, LLC.
Alexandra Sologub of WFBM, LLP (714-634-2522), appearing for defendants, General Electric Company and ViacomCBS, Inc.

RULING:
Not contested, not argued and tentative ruling adopted:
Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). Defendant Amcord, Inc. withdrew its opposition.
The trial date is June 13, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is June 24, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.
The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 9, 2022. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 27, 2022. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 10, 2022. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than, March 25, 2022. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than March 10, 2022, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than March 10, 2022, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for April 28, 2022 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than April 29, 2022. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 13, 2022. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by June 3, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, June 9, 2022 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Not reported. (503/CML)

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Set For Trial On Sep-12-22 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503
